response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, mobile, and backend credentials, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU architecture work, SIMD/MIMD experience, deep learning workload optimization, or silicon-level performance analysis. The educational background (BS in CS) combined with general software experience does not align with a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware.
strengths: Strong general software engineering experience across multiple languages and stacks
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Brief exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep learning training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack, not hardware-software co-design or systems architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep learning workload optimization background
Some low-level C/C++ and performance-sensitive systems exposure
